options.name: --homedir
options.description: Set the name of the home directory

options.name: --options
options.description: Read options from file

name: -a
--armour
options.description: Create ASCII armored output

name: -o
--output
options.description: Write output to file

name: -u
--local-user
options.description: Use name as the user ID to sign

options.name: --default-key
options.description: Use name as default user ID for signatures

name: -r
--recipient
options.description: Encrypt for user id name

options.name: --default-recipient
options.description: Use name as default recipient

options.name: --default-recipient-self
options.description: Use the default key as default recipient

options.name: --no-default-recipient
options.description: Reset --default-recipient and --default-recipient-self

options.name: --encrypt-to
options.description: Same as --recipient but this one is intended for in the options file

options.name: --no-encrypt-to
options.description: Disable the use of all --encrypt-to keys

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Give more information during processing

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Try to be as quiet as possible

options.name: -Z
options.description: Set compression level to n

name: -t
--textmode
options.description: Use canonical text mode

name: -n
--dry-run
options.description: Don't make any changes

name: -i
--interactive
options.description: Prompt before overwriting any files

options.name: --batch
options.description: Use batch mode

options.name: --no-tty
options.description: Make sure that the TTY is never used for any output

options.name: --no-batch
options.description: Disable batch mode

options.name: --yes
options.description: Assume "yes" on most questions

options.name: --no
options.description: Assume "no" on most questions

options.name: --always-trust
options.description: Skip key validation

options.name: --keyserver
options.description: Use name to lookup keys which are not yet in your keyring

options.name: --no-auto-key-retrieve
options.description: Disables the automatic retrieving of keys

options.name: --honor-http-proxy
options.description: Try to access the keyserver over the proxy

options.name: --keyring
options.description: Add file to the list of keyrings

options.name: --secret-keyring
options.description: Same as --keyring but for the secret keyrings

options.name: --charset
options.description: Set the name of the native character set

options.name: --utf8-strings
options.description: Assume that the arguments are already given as UTF8

options.name: --no-options
options.description: Shortcut for "--options /dev/null"

options.name: --load-extension
options.description: Load an extension module

args.name: name
args.description: If name does not contain a slash it is searched in "/usr/local/lib/gnupg"

options.name: --debug
options.description: Set debugging flags

options.name: --debug-all
options.description: Set all useful debugging flags

options.name: --status-fd
options.description: Write special status strings to the file descriptor n

options.name: --logger-fd
options.description: Write log output to file descriptor n and not to stderr

options.name: --no-comment
options.description: Do not write comment packets

options.name: --comment
options.description: Use string as comment string in clear text signatures

options.name: --default-comment
options.description: Force to write the standard comment string

options.name: --no-version
options.description: Omit the version string in clear text signatures

options.name: --emit-version
options.description: Force to write the version string

name: -N
--notation-data
options.description: Put the name value pair into the signature as notation data

options.name: --set-policy-url
options.description: Use string as Policy URL for signatures (rfc2440:5.2.3.19)

options.name: --set-filename
options.description: Use string as the name of file which is stored in messages

options.name: --use-embedded-filename
options.description: Try to create a file with a name as embedded in the data

options.name: --completes-needed
options.description: Number of completely trusted users to introduce a new key signer

options.name: --marginals-needed
options.description: Number of marginally trusted users to introduce a new key signer

options.name: --max-cert-depth
options.description: Maximum depth of a certification chain

options.name: --cipher-algo
options.description: Use name as cipher algorithm

options.name: --digest-algo
options.description: Use name as message digest algorithm

options.name: --s2k-cipher-algo
options.description: Use name as the cipher algorithm used to protect secret keys

options.name: --s2k-digest-algo
options.description: Use name as the digest algorithm used to mangle the passphrases

options.name: --s2k-mode
options.description: Selects how passphrases are mangled

options.name: --compress-algo
options.description: Use compress algorithm n

options.name: --disable-cipher-algo
options.description: Never allow the use of name as cipher algorithm

options.name: --disable-pubkey-algo
options.description: Never allow the use of name as public key algorithm

options.name: --throw-keyid
options.description: Do not put the keyid into encrypted packets

options.name: --not-dash-escaped
options.description: Makes signatures usable for patch files

options.name: --escape-from-lines
options.description: Handle "<From" in emails

options.name: --passphrase-fd
options.description: Read the passphrase from file descriptor n

options.name: --command-fd
options.description: This is a replacement for the depreciated shared-memory IPC mode

options.name: --rfc1991
options.description: Try to be more RFC1991 (PGP 2.x) compliant

options.name: --openpgp
options.description: Reset all packet, cipher and digest options to OpenPGP

options.name: --force-v3-sigs
options.description: Forces v3 signatures for signatures on data

options.name: --force-mdc
options.description: Force encryption with appended manipulation code

options.name: --allow-non-selfsigned-uid
options.description: Allow non self-signed user IDs

options.name: --allow-freeform-uid
options.description: Disable all checks on the form of the user ID

options.name: --ignore-time-conflict
options.description: Makes time checks just a warning

options.name: --lock-once
options.description: Do not release the lock until the process terminates

options.name: --lock-multiple
options.description: Release the locks every time one is no longer needed

options.name: --lock-never
options.description: Disable locking entirely

options.name: --no-random-seed-file
options.description: Disable random pool cache

options.name: --no-verbose
options.description: Reset verbose level to 0

options.name: --no-greeting
options.description: Suppress the initial copyright message

options.name: --no-secmem-warning
options.description: Suppress the "using insecure memory" warning

options.name: --no-armor
options.description: Assume the input data is not in ASCII armored format

options.name: --no-default-keyring
options.description: Do not add the keyrings to the list of keyrings

options.name: --skip-verify
options.description: Skip the signature verification step

options.name: --with-colons
options.description: Print key listings delimited by colons

options.name: --with-key-data
options.description: --with-colons with the public key data

options.name: --with-fingerprint
options.description: --fingerprint with a different format

options.name: --fast-list-mode
options.description: Changes the list commands to work faster

options.name: --list-only
options.description: Skip the decryption pass

options.name: --no-literal
options.description: This is not for normal use

options.name: --set-filesize
options.description: This is not for normal use

options.name: --emulate-md-encode-bug
options.description: Enables workaround to check faulty signatures

options.name: --show-session-key
options.description: Display the session key used for one message

options.name: --override-session-key
options.description: Don't use the public key but the session key string

options.name: --merge-only
options.description: Don't insert new keys into the keyrings

options.name: --try-all-secrets
options.description: Try all secret keys in turn to find the right one

name: -s
--sign
subcommands.description: Make a signature

subcommands.name: --clearsign
subcommands.description: Make a clear text signature

name: -b
--detach-sign
subcommands.description: Make a detached signature

name: -e
--encrypt
subcommands.description: Encrypt data

name: -c
--symmetric
subcommands.description: Encrypt with symmetric cipher only

subcommands.name: --store
subcommands.description: Store only (make a simple RFC1991 packet)

subcommands.name: --decrypt
subcommands.description: Decrypt file (or stdin if no file is specified) and write it to stdout

subcommands.name: --verify
subcommands.description: Assume that sigfile is a signature and verify it without generating any output

subcommands.name: --verify-files
subcommands.description: Special version of --verify which does not work with detached signatures

name: --list-keys
--list-public-keys
subcommands.description: List all keys from public keyrings, or just the ones given on the command line

subcommands.name: --list-secret-keys
subcommands.description: List all keys from secret keyrings, or just the ones given on the command line

subcommands.name: --list-sigs
subcommands.description: Same as --list-keys, but the signatures are listed too

subcommands.name: --check-sigs
subcommands.description: Same as --list-sigs, but the signatures are verified

subcommands.name: --fingerprint
subcommands.description: List all keys with their fingerprints

subcommands.name: --list-packets
subcommands.description: List only the sequence of packets

subcommands.name: --gen-key
subcommands.description: Generate a new key pair

subcommands.name: --edit-key
subcommands.description: Present a menu which enables you to do all key related tasks

subcommands.name: --sign-key
subcommands.description: Sign a public key with you secret key

subcommands.name: --lsign-key
subcommands.description: Sign a public key with you secret key but mark it as non-exportable

subcommands.name: --trusted-key
subcommands.description: Assume that the specified key is as trustworthy as one of your own secret keys

subcommands.name: --delete-key
subcommands.description: Remove key from the public keyring

subcommands.name: --delete-secret-key
subcommands.description: Remove key from the secret and public keyring

subcommands.name: --gen-revoke
subcommands.description: Generate a revocation certificate for the complete key

subcommands.name: --export
subcommands.description: Either export all keys from all keyrings or those of the given names

subcommands.name: --send-keys
subcommands.description: Same as --export but sends the keys to a keyserver

subcommands.name: --export-all
subcommands.description: Same as --export, but also exports keys which are not compatible to OpenPGP

subcommands.name: --export-secret-keys
subcommands.description: Same as --export, but exports the secret keys

subcommands.name: --export-secret-subkeys
subcommands.description: Same as --export, but exports the secret subkeys

subcommands.name: --import
subcommands.description: Import/merge keys. This adds the given keys to the keyring

subcommands.name: --fast-import
subcommands.description: --import but does not build the trustdb

subcommands.name: --recv-keys
subcommands.description: Import the keys with the given key IDs from a HKP keyserver

subcommands.name: --export-ownertrust
subcommands.description: List the ownertrust values in ASCII format

subcommands.name: --import-ownertrust
subcommands.description: Update the trustdb with the ownertrust values stored in files

subcommands.name: --print-md
subcommands.description: Print message digest of algorithm ALGO for all given files of stdin

subcommands.name: --gen-random
subcommands.description: Emit COUNT random bytes of the given quality level

subcommands.name: --gen-prime
subcommands.description: Generate prime numbers

subcommands.name: --version
subcommands.description: Print version information

subcommands.name: --warranty
subcommands.description: Print warranty information

name: -h
--help
subcommands.description: Print usage information
